div.p:
c = a × b Where: a = 3.0 b = 2.0
c = 6.0
a.grad += b.data × c.grad b.grad += a.data × c.grad
python def _backward(): a.grad += b.data * c.grad b.grad += a.data * c.grad
python def _backward(): a.grad += b.grad * c.grad # BUG! b.grad += a.grad * c.grad # BUG!
This would use gradient, not value

g.text: other = other if isinstance(other, Value) else Value(other)

div.p: The local derivative of multiplication is the value of the other operand:

ul.li: ∂(a×b)/∂a = b — b is treated as a constant

We use b.data (the constant value), not b.grad (how b's output changes) The gradient b.grad tells us how loss changes with respect to b. But here, we need: "how does c change when a changes, holding b fixed?" That's b.data — the numeric value of b.
